title: Continuous Authentication Compliance
spl:
index=identity sourcetype="azure:signin" earliest=-7d
| where risk_level!="none" OR authentication_requirement="multiFactorAuthentication"
| stats count by user, risk_detail, result
| sort 40 -count
description: Tracks step-up auth, re-auth, and session risk evaluation outcomes for policies requiring continuous verification.
value: Zero Trust architects measure continuous authentication compliance by tracking mid-session re-authentication triggers, token lifetimes, and CAE enforcement, ensuring access is continuously re-evaluated rather than granted once and trusted forever.
implementation: Map Entra `riskLevelDuringSignIn` and CA grant controls. Report MFA completion rate when risk elevated.
visualization: Table (risky sign-ins), Bar chart (outcomes), Line chart (risk events / day).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Continuous authentication signals from IdP (Entra ID, Okta) and ZTNA platforms. Entra ID: `sourcetype=azure:aad:signin` with `properties.authenticationRequirement` and Continuous Access Evaluation (CAE) events. Okta: `sourcetype=okta:im2:log` with session and re-auth events. Key fields: `properties.tokenIssuedAt`, `properties.authenticationProcessingDetails`, `properties.resourceDisplayName`.
• Zero Trust requires continuous verification, not one-time login. Continuous Authentication means: session tokens are re-evaluated when risk signals change (IP change, device compliance change, user risk change). Entra ID CAE revokes tokens in near-real-time when conditions change.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify CAE events:
```spl
index=azure sourcetype="azure:aad:signin" earliest=-7d
| where match(properties.authenticationProcessingDetails{}.key, "(?i)continuous|cae")
| stats count by properties.authenticationRequirement
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Re-authentication triggers:
```spl
index=azure sourcetype="azure:aad:signin" earliest=-7d
| where properties.authenticationRequirement!="singleFactorAuthentication"
| eval reauth_reason=coalesce(properties.authenticationRequirement, "unknown")
| timechart span=1d count by reauth_reason
```

Understanding this SPL: Trends re-authentication events by reason. 'multiFactorAuthentication' triggered mid-session means a risk signal changed (location shift, device non-compliance). CAE-triggered re-auth means Entra ID revoked the token and forced a new sign-in. Frequent re-auth for the same user may indicate their device is oscillating between compliant and non-compliant states.

Session lifetime analysis:
```spl
index=azure sourcetype="azure:aad:signin" earliest=-7d
| eval token_age_hours=round((now() - strptime(properties.tokenIssuedAt, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")) / 3600, 1)
| stats avg(token_age_hours) as avg_token_age max(token_age_hours) as max_token_age by properties.resourceDisplayName
| sort - max_token_age
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Change a device's compliance state. Verify re-authentication is triggered.
(b) Check Entra ID Sign-in Logs for CAE enforcement events.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Re-auth trigger trend. Row 2 — Session/token age distribution. Row 3 — Users with excessive re-auth.
Alert: When max token age exceeds 24 hours for any Tier-1 application.

Runbook:
1. **Long-lived tokens** — Application may not support CAE. Check app registration settings.
2. **Excessive re-auth for one user** — Device compliance flapping. Fix device posture.
3. **No CAE events** — CAE may not be enabled. Check Entra ID Security > Conditional Access > Session.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No re-auth events** — Continuous access evaluation may not be configured. Enable CAE in Entra ID.
• **Token age always 0** — `tokenIssuedAt` may not be in the expected format. Check field extraction.
grandmaExplanation: In Zero Trust, checking your identity once at login is not enough — we keep checking throughout the day. If your device becomes insecure or your location changes suspiciously, the system forces you to prove who you are again. We track how often this happens and make sure no session tokens live too long.
